bands.label: low
bands.text:
You often doubt your own worth and tend to see more flaws in yourself than strengths. Criticism, even minor, can feel like confirmation of these doubts.

This is a common and not permanent state — self-esteem can change over time and with experience. Practicing noticing your own achievements, even small ones, and working with a professional can meaningfully improve this feeling.

bands.label: below average
bands.text:
You have moderate doubts about your own worth, though you feel more confident in stable, supportive conditions.

This leaves room for growth — focusing on your own strengths and achievements gradually strengthens this feeling.

bands.label: above average
bands.text:
You generally accept yourself and recognize your own worth, though you sometimes doubt yourself in difficult or stressful situations.

This is a healthy, solid base of self-esteem with a little room to grow more confident amid unpredictable circumstances.

bands.label: high
bands.text:
You confidently accept yourself, recognize your own worth, and rarely doubt your own dignity, even in the face of criticism or setbacks.

This is a solid psychological foundation that supports resilience in the face of hardship. It's worth keeping a healthy dose of humility so that confidence doesn't turn into inattention to feedback from others.

questions.text: On the whole, I'm satisfied with myself.
I feel that I have a number of good qualities.
I'm able to do things as well as most other people.
I take a positive attitude toward myself.
I rarely feel that I am worthless.

portraits.title: Grounded Confidence
portraits.text: You accept yourself, strengths and weaknesses alike, maintaining a stable inner foundation even in difficult situations. This isn't arrogance — it's a calm recognition of your own worth.

portraits.title: Growing Self-Esteem
portraits.text: You're at a stage where self-doubt shows up more often than confidence. This isn't a permanent state — deliberately working on noticing your own achievements and, if needed, support from a professional can meaningfully change this picture over time.
